checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present and substantive

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 3 flows: flow_1 (Campaign Measurement, 13 nodes/13 edges), flow_2 (Outlier Detection, 9 nodes/10 edges), flow_3 (Error Recovery, 9 nodes/11 edges). All well-connected.

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 1 schedule trigger: 0 9 * * * daily at 9 AM. Valid cron.

checks.name: connector_role_alignment
checks.detail: mailchimp=email_delivery, airtable=knowledge_base, slack=chat_messaging — all valid roles

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 5 adoption_questions added: slack_channel, airtable_base_id, airtable_table_name, outlier_threshold, mailchimp_dc

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. Read-only operations on Mailchimp. No destructive actions.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 60+ words. Instructions cover 6 numbered steps with measurement windows, benchmark logic, Airtable schema, Slack formatting, and memory management. Tool guidance provides Mailchimp, Airtable, Slack API endpoints. 3 examples covering normal, outlier, and no-op scenarios. Error handling covers per-service failures and recovery.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request mapped to all 3 service APIs with specific endpoints. Mailchimp datacenter ({dc}) handling documented. No phantom tools. Minor: tool guidance could include agent_memory protocol calls explicitly.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Daily 9 AM trigger matches the measurement window logic (checking 24h and 72h windows). Solid alignment. Could benefit from a second trigger for retries after failures.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 service_flow entries backed by connectors with complete credential fields and detailed setup instructions.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Agent memory stores rolling benchmarks per audience (20-campaign window), processed campaign tracking, and error recovery state. Schema documented in JSON. Good design.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Covers Mailchimp 401/403/429/404, Airtable 422/429, Slack failures, duplicate detection, missing benchmarks, partial data, and mid-pipeline recovery. Very thorough.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 use_case_flows fully covered: primary measurement cycle, outlier detection sub-workflow, and error recovery flow. Prompt descriptions match flow diagrams precisely.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear outcome: automated campaign performance monitoring with benchmark-based insights. Replaces 4 separate workflows. Dual-window measurement is a concrete differentiator.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Mailchimp, Airtable, Slack APIs are standard. Mailchimp DC-based routing adds slight complexity. All configs actionable.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Rolling benchmarks, outlier detection, dual-window analysis, contextual trend reporting — substantial reasoning beyond simple automation.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 5 questions are genuinely needed. mailchimp_dc is essential for API routing. outlier_threshold allows tuning sensitivity.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Good defaults for slack_channel (#marketing-analytics), table_name (Campaign Performance), outlier_threshold (15%), mailchimp_dc (us19). airtable_base_id must be user-provided.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — 5 added covering Slack routing, Airtable config, outlier tuning, and Mailchimp DC
Mailchimp and Airtable connectors may not have builtin definitions

template: Added 5 adoption_questions: slack_channel, airtable_base_id, airtable_table_name, outlier_threshold, mailchimp_dc
Included select type with options for outlier_threshold for constrained choice
